data.description: You tune your mind to the beat of the natural world. On a successful check, you gain advantage on the next ability check you make to notice something in the environment or to track a creature. On a critical success, you learn the exact location and nature of one visible creature within 1 mile.
A creature must duel a target of your choice.

You can attempt to compel a creature you see within range to make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is drawn to you and must use its reaction (if any) to make an attack against another hostile creature other than you or disadvantage a Wisdom saving throw each time it attempts to move to a space that is more than 30 feet away from you; if it succeeds on this save, this doesn't stop it from moving. The spell ends on your turn if you cast a harmful spell on any creature, or if you end your turn more than 90 feet from the target.
You understand the spoken and written words of a language you don't know, if they are being used within 30 feet of you. This spell can be cast on yourself or on one willing creature that you touch.

Each blast cold erupts your creature a cone making Constitution saving throw on A creature taking cold A throw. creature 8d8 damage a failed or as damage on successful one. save, half much. A creature killed this becomes a frozen statue until thaws. At Higher LeveIs. When you cast this speIl using a spell of level higher, damage increases by the slot 6th or ld8 each leveI 5th.

A beam of blue light strikes a target creature within range and blinds it until the start of your next turn, or until you or a friendly creature speaks soothing words to it. While blinded by this spell, the target must also make a Wisdom saving throw at the end of each of its turns (DC 14). On a failed save, it wastes its action using a random limb (such as flailing arms) with no effective purpose; on a success, it can use that action in any normal manner.

This spell assaults creatures' minds, spawning delusions provoking uncontrolled action. Each sphere centered on a point within range must succeed a Wisdom saving throw when cast; affected targets can't take reactions and at the start of each of their turns must understand the meaning of any spoken language they hear as literal gibberish, while also taking 5d10 psychic damage. For the spell's duration, you determine its behavior that you understand spoken and written language only if touching a surface with words written on it.
